purpose: Day-7 EXPANDED external drug-target truth set. Pre-registration of the label-generation RULE, frozen BEFORE any gene receives a label. Label-gathering half of the powered external validation; scoring (AUROC) is done separately in the main kernel.
relationship_to_iter4: Faithful expansion of day6_external_validation (artifact a4978434, receipt sha256 9ddcdc0e...). SAME external truth source (Open Targets drugAndClinicalCandidates), SAME pre-registered autoimmune indication list, SAME tier2/tier1 definitions. Only the SCORED GENE UNIVERSE changes: 44 -> 98 eligible genes (T1D-associated, perturbed in Marson screen, NOT gold/axis-forbidden), to obtain more external positives and tighten the marginal iter4 win.
scored_universe: 98 eligible genes provided at handoff/eligible_universe.json (symbol, ensembl, ot_t1d, ot_t2d).
id_remap_note: The 13 EFO ids used in iter4 no longer resolve in the current Open Targets release (returned disease:null on 2026-07-11); they were remapped by OT to MONDO. Each was re-resolved by exact name via OT search (entityNames=[disease], top hit). The two MONDO ids (T1D, MS) still resolve unchanged. This is a platform-version remap of the SAME indications, not a change of the indication list. Documented for full traceability.
stage_map_rationale: tier2 = 'maxClinicalStage >= Phase I' (iter4 wording). EARLY_PHASE_1 (Phase 0), IND, PRECLINICAL, UNKNOWN are NOT counted as clinical (numeric 0) to stay faithful to '>= Phase I'. This is conservative and pre-registered before scoring.

tier_definitions.tier2_PRIMARY_any_clinical: gene is the mechanistic MoA target of >=1 drug whose maxClinicalStage for >=1 pre-registered autoimmune indication is >= PHASE_1 (numeric>=1).
tier_definitions.tier1_STRICT_approved_or_late: gene is the MoA target of >=1 drug that is APPROVAL or PHASE_3+ (numeric>=3) for >=1 pre-registered autoimmune indication.

matching_logic: FAITHFUL to iter4. Step 1 (indication drug pool): for each of the 15 pre-registered indications, pull Open Targets Disease.drugAndClinicalCandidates (server-side expands each indication to its ontology descendants, e.g. cutaneous lupus under SLE); record each drug's maxClinicalStage FOR THAT INDICATION. Pool across indications keeping the max stage per drug. Step 2 (MoA edge): for each of the 98 genes, pull Open Targets Target.drugAndClinicalCandidates to get the set of drugs for which the gene is a mechanistic target. Step 3 (label): gene is tier2-POSITIVE iff intersection(gene's MoA drugs, pooled-indication drugs with stage_numeric>=1) is non-empty; tier1-POSITIVE iff the intersecting drug has pooled stage_numeric>=3. The disease-side pool defines 'developed for a pre-registered autoimmune indication (incl. descendants)'; the target-side edge defines 'gene is the drug's MoA target'. This is exactly the iter4 construct, only the scored gene universe is expanded 44->98.
is_external: Labels use ONLY drug/clinical-trial precedent — a datasource the gate/adjudication rule never used (it used immune-GO, OT-T1D>T2D, GWAS-tag, measured Treg direction). A validation that CAN fail.
honesty_contract: Labels frozen + hashed BEFORE any AUROC/scoring. No tuning, no cherry-picking. Negatives/nulls reported plainly. All positives traced to OT drug rows.
cross_check: Target-side per-drug disease annotations (Target.drugAndClinicalCandidates.diseases) are retained as an independent cross-check that the intersecting drug is annotated to the indication.

sensitivity_tier_immune_mechanism.name: y_tier2_immune
sensitivity_tier_immune_mechanism.definition: tier2-positive AND >=1 of the gene's driving drugs is immunomodulatory, where a drug is immunomodulatory iff >=1 of its Open Targets mechanism-of-action canonical targets is in a fixed reference set of immune-system drug targets (listed in immune_target_reference_set). Complements the iter4 'exclude pure-metabolic distractors' sensitivity set with an objective, drug-MoA-based criterion applied uniformly to all 98 genes.
sensitivity_tier_immune_mechanism.timing_disclosure: The immune reference set is defined from canonical immunomodulatory drug-target biology, NOT from any gene's label or the gate's GO/WITHHOLD call. It was written after observing that the literal rule's positive class is dominated by metabolic-drug annotations, so it is reported strictly as a pre-registered SENSITIVITY analysis, never as the primary. The literal iter4 tier2 rule remains the frozen PRIMARY label.

honesty_finding_metformin_artifact: PLAIN REPORT: the literal tier2 rule yields 56/98 positives, but 38 of them are positive ONLY because Open Targets annotates METFORMIN's mechanism-of-action target as the entire mitochondrial complex-I subunit list (the NDUF gene family), and metformin is in >=Phase I trials for T1D/vitiligo. These 38 NDUF positives therefore share a SINGLE drug-MoA edge and are not independent external evidence. This is exactly the metabolic-passenger confound the project's gate is designed to reject. The count is reported as-is (no tuning, no cherry-picking); the metformin_only_complexI flag and the immune-mechanism sensitivity tier (7 positives) are frozen alongside so the scoring kernel can report the primary AND the mechanism-independent result honestly.
consistency_check_vs_iter4: Of iter4's 5 tier2 positives (CD28,CD6,ICOS,PPARG,PRKCQ), the two present in this 98-gene universe (CD6, PPARG) are both positive here. CD6 is in the immune sensitivity tier; PPARG is positive via mesalamine/balsalazide (IBD) exactly as in iter4.
